title: How Solar Panels Make Electricity
script_summary: Explains the photovoltaic effect that allows solar panels to generate electricity.

segments.voiceover: Photons knock electrons loose in silicon — that's the photovoltaic effect.
But what does that really mean for energy generation?
In a solar panel, sunlight hits silicon cells, exciting electrons.
These moving electrons create an electric current.
And here’s the kicker: it’s all about the material!
Silicon’s unique properties make it a superstar in solar tech.
Curious about renewable energy? Hit follow for more!

In a solar panel, sunlight hits silicon cells, exciting electrons. These moving electrons create an electric current. And here’s the kicker: it’s all about the material! Silicon’s unique properties make it a superstar in solar tech. Curious about renewable energy? Hit follow for more!
